title: Switch Statement
description: Use a switch statement to handle multiple specific cases for a variable value.
problemStatement:
Switch cases are clean! Given `$color = "red"`, write a `switch` statement that prints `"Stop"` for `"red"` and `"Go"` for `"green"`.

**Requirement:** Include the `break;` statement for each case.
hints: Include a `default` case to handle unexpected values.
solutionCode:
<?php
$color = "red";
switch ($color) {
    case "red":
        echo "Stop";
        break;
    case "green":
        echo "Go";
        break;
    default:
        echo "Unknown";
}
?>
